items.description: Post-medieval cursive inscription in a flowing later hand reading 'Jorlaugur f. a. f.' written across the lower margin beneath the final line of the main text.
items.scholarly_note: 17th–19th century ownership, attribution or scholarly annotation by an Icelandic hand. 'Jorlaugur' is a documented Icelandic personal name; 'f. a. f.' is likely an abbreviation (filiation, 'fæddur á', or formulaic phrase). Such notes commonly appear in Codex Regius after its 1643 transfer to Denmark.

items.description: Ornate pen-drawn symbolic flourish resembling a quatrefoil, cross with looped or bulbous terminals, or stylized floral/cross mark placed immediately after or integrated with the cursive inscription.
items.scholarly_note: Decorative mark or personal sigil by the same post-medieval hand. Not medieval, not figurative (no face, animal or grotesque), and not a pen trial. Serves to highlight or authenticate the adjacent inscription.

items.description: Arabic numeral '27.' written small and neat in the upper right corner outside the text block.
items.scholarly_note: Early modern foliation. Codex Regius received systematic foliation after entering the Danish royal collection in 1643; this mark belongs to that later numbering sequence (project context labels the leaf page 36).

items.description: Irregular brownish discoloration and diffuse staining along the left edge extending into the upper left margin and outer text area.
items.scholarly_note: Typical age, handling and environmental staining (foxing/accumulated grime) on calf vellum. Does not affect legibility of the text.

items.description: Brownish age stains and scattered darker spots in the lower right margin near the page edge.
items.scholarly_note: Cumulative wear and minor moisture or handling marks. The lower margin also exhibits slight natural cockling of the parchment.

items.description: Modestly enlarged capital 'N' opening the visible section ('Ne þ...'), with a slightly curved stem and small terminal flourish on the left.
items.scholarly_note: Standard structural decoration used by the main scribe to mark the start of a syntactic or stanzaic unit in the Eddic poem. Not a full historiated or rubricated initial.

items.description: Minor local variations in ink density and letter formation (thickened or reinforced strokes) visible on a small number of letters in the upper and middle text block, including areas near words containing 'hafpi' and adjacent minims.
items.scholarly_note: Scribal self-correction or reinforcement of strokes during copying, typical of iron-gall ink work. No expunctions, strikethroughs, or interlinear/marginal insertions are present.

damage_notes: Edge wear and irregular parchment trimming, especially visible on left and lower margins
Minor natural cockling and surface texture of the vellum, consistent with high-quality but aged calfskin
Localized iron-gall ink fading and slight corrosion in a few letters, typical of 13th-century Icelandic production
No holes, worming, or significant tears within the writing area; parchment remains intact
summary: Page 36 (Völundarkviða section) of Codex Regius is largely clean within the text block, written in the principal Gothic book hand of c. 1270. The only notable non-text additions are a post-medieval cursive inscription 'Jorlaugur f. a. f.' together with an ornate symbolic flourish in the lower margin, plus the early modern foliation '27.' in the upper right. Age-related brownish staining appears along the left edge and lower right margin. No figurative doodles, pen trials, interlinear glosses, animals, faces, or major physical damage are present. Minor ink reinforcement by the original scribe occurs in isolated letters but does not constitute systematic correction or later intervention.
